title: taraxacum brachyglossoides
intro: Taraxacum brachyglossoides, also known as the lesser hawk's beard or short-bearded dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Taraxacum brachyglossoides has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in capsule or powder form, to support overall health and well-being.
